name: Natural Site Torcal de Antequera via Green and Yellow routes (SL-A 295)
name_original: Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera por rutas Verde y Amarilla (SL-A 295)
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera navigate a distinctive karst landscape, characterized by towering limestone formations and a high-altitude plateau over 1200 meters above sea level. This region features unique geological structures, formed by millions of years of erosion, creating a natural labyrinth of rock. The terrain includes varied paths through these formations, offering views across the surrounding area. Best easy hiking trails around Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera The most popular easy hiking route is The Torcal of Antequera – Ammonite Fossils in Rock Walls loop from Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera, a 0.8 miles (1.3 km) trail that takes 20 minutes to complete. This path leads through the unique karst formations and past ancient ammonite fossils embedded in the rock. Another top favourite among local hikers is Petroglyph of a Spiral Carving loop from Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera, an easy 0.7 miles (1.1 km) path. This route offers views of the distinctive rock formations and includes a small elevation gain of 114 feet (35 meters). Local hikers also love the Dolmens of Antequera – Dolmen de Viera loop from Antequera Dolmens Site, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera is defined by its surreal karst formations, diverse flora and fauna, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 45 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 750 reviews. More than 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera?
A: There are 45 easy hiking trails available in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera, offering a variety of options to explore the unique karst landscape.

Q: What makes the landscape of Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera unique for easy hikes?
A: The region is renowned for its extraordinary karst landscape, often described as a 'stone city.' Millions of years of erosion have created towering, intricately shaped limestone rock formations, offering a surreal and otherworldly experience even on easy paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy easy hikes in El Torcal?
A: El Torcal can be visited year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. Due to its altitude, over 1200 meters, temperatures can be significantly lower than in nearby towns, and fog is frequent, adding to its mystical atmosphere. Clear nights make it excellent for stargazing as it's a Starlight Reserve.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see on an easy hike in El Torcal?
A: Despite its rocky appearance, El Torcal is rich in biodiversity. You are likely to encounter mountain goats (Spanish ibex) up close, as they are accustomed to hikers. The park also supports a wide variety of birds and almost 700 different plant species.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are circular. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38271241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Torcal of Antequera – Ammonite Fossils in Rock Walls loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40392534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Petroglyph of a Spiral Carving loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy trails in El Torcal suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many easy trails are very suitable for families. The Green Route, for instance, is a 1.5 km loop designed for families and beginners. The unique rock formations and potential for wildlife spotting make it an engaging experience for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera?
A: While the official Green and Yellow routes within the core protected area of El Torcal generally do not permit dogs, there are often surrounding trails and areas where dogs on a leash are allowed. It's always best to check local signage or the park's official website for the most current regulations regarding pets.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the easy hiking trails?
A: Along the easy trails, you can marvel at the unique karst formations, often resembling stacks or figures. Notable features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/230042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Torcal of Antequera&lt;/a&gt; itself, and specific formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3529822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cathedrals of El Torcal&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by ancient ammonite fossils embedded in the rock.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 750 reviews. Reviewers often praise the surreal and unique geological formations, the well-marked paths, and the opportunity to see mountain goats up close.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several easy hikes provide stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21831864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Torcal Screw – View of Antequera loop&lt;/a&gt; offers vistas of the surrounding landscape. The Mirador de las Ventanillas viewpoint, accessible via some trails, provides impressive views, including Málaga and, on clear days, even Africa.

Q: Is there parking available for easy hikes in El Torcal?
A: Yes, there is parking available at the visitor center, which serves as a starting point for many of the well-known trails, including the easy Green and Yellow routes. During peak times, the upper car park may fill up, and a shuttle service might operate from a lower car park.

Q: Are there any cafes or amenities near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, there is a visitor center at El Torcal which typically includes a cafe or restaurant where you can get refreshments and food before or after your hike. It also offers facilities like restrooms and information about the park.

Q: What should I wear for an easy hike in Paraje Natural Torcal de Antequera?
A: Given the altitude and potential for changing weather, even on easy trails, it's advisable to wear layers. Sturdy walking shoes are recommended due to the rocky and uneven terrain. Bring water, sun protection, and a light jacket, as strong winds and fog can occur.

Access to the Torcal Alto Visitor Center is free and it has an interpretive area, multipurpose room, guide service, observatory, souvenir shop, cafeteria-restaurant, toilets and parking.
